{{Quote|You will transmit the following orders to the fleet—tell each admiral to deploy his squadron to the Vento system shipyards at the following coordinates—07.882.76.999—|Ulic Qel-Droma, to the commander of Coruscant's war room|<ref name="TotJ" />}}
The '''Vento system shipyards''', also known as the '''Vento shipyards''', were ancient [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] that were located within the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Vento system/Legends|Vento system]] and lay on the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] known as the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]]. The shipyards were used by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]—a conflict waged by the forces of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] sect against the Republic—and came under a [[Attack on the Vento system shipyards|Sith attack]] during their first offensive wave.

During the final stages of the war, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|attempted to force]] a [[Unidentified commander (Coruscant's war room)|Republic military officer]] to transmit orders to the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic fleet]] instructing all the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to arrive at the coordinates of the Vento [[Star system/Legends|system]] shipyards—an action that would cause the destruction of many Republic vessels. Qel-Droma's orders were not executed, however, due to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] being arrested by a group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. The Vento shipyards were still in existence by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of later conflicts such as the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and the [[Jedi Civil War]].

==Description==
The [[Vento system/Legends|Vento system]] [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]],<ref name="TotJ" /> also known as the Vento shipyards,<ref name="TEA" /> were located within the Vento system<ref name="TotJ" /> of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]<ref name="EAOC" /> and lay on the [[Super-hyperroute/Legends|super-hyperroute]] known as the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]].<ref name="TEA" />

==History==
{{Quote|Are you insane? If I give them all the same coordinates, they'll demolish each other!|The commander of Coruscant's war room, to Ulic Qel-Droma|<ref name="TotJ" />}}
Considered to be ancient, the Vento system shipyards were used by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="CSWE" /> for constructing or repairing [[Starship/Legends|starships]] by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]—a conflict waged by the forces of the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] sect against the Republic in [[3996 BBY]]. During that war, the shipyards were the site of a [[Attack on the Vento system shipyards|Sith attack]] during their first offensive wave.<ref name="TEA" />

When the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|attacked]] the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]<ref name="TotJ" /> in 3,996 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="TEA" /> he entered the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|city-world's]] [[Republic command center|command center]] and tried to force its [[Unidentified commander (Coruscant's war room)|commander]] to transmit orders to the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic fleet]] instructing all the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] vessels to arrive at the same exact coordinates—those of the Vento system shipyards. Such an action was predicted by the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] to cause a collision of the Republic's starships that would lead to the destruction of many vessels; however the execution of Qel-Droma's orders was prevented by the arrival of a group of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] who arrested the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]].<ref name="TotJ" />

The Vento shipyards were still used by the Republic during the [[Mandalorian Wars]], which were fought between the Republic and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]] from [[3976 BBY|3976]] to [[3960 BBY]], and the [[Jedi Civil War]]—a conflict between the Republic and the Dark Lord of the Sith [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|empire]] that lasted from [[3959 BBY|3959]] to [[3956 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Vento system shipyards were first mentioned in the [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|second issue]] of [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War|''The Sith War'']] story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] series of [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]]. The issue was written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]], penciled by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], and released by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="TotJ" /> on [[September 19]], [[1995]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54596/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_-_the_sith_war_1995_2|text=Star Wars: Tales Of The Jedi - The Sith War (1995) #2}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the shipyards in grid square L-9.<ref name="TEA" />
